steps.title: Build the line from the intercept and one point
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is $(0,-16)$, and the table gives the point $(-12,44)$. Enter those two points and run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=-5$ and $b=-16$, so the line is $y=-5x-16$.

steps.title: Use the graph to read a and b
steps.content: Graph $y=-5x-16$ in Desmos. For $a$, the table row has $y=14$, so graph $y=14$ and read the intersection: $x=-6$, so $a=-6$. For $b$, use the row where $x=2$: $y=-5(2)-16=-26$, so $b=-26$.
